parsed_review.summary: CP00-440 is a descriptor-only settlement-core closeout slice (RP14.P05.M06.S04-RP14.P06.M03.S02, 150 units, risk C) that extends the established CP426-CP439 pattern. Independent read-only inspection of all eight active implementation refs (registry.js, service.js, validators.js, index.js, contract JSON, model.test.js, validate script, README) found them fully consistent. Counts verified: deliverable_counts {fixture:31, claude_review:7, implementation:58, security_audit:21, test:18, hermes_evidence:4, ui:11} and micro_title_row_counts both sum to 150 and match the prompt baseline; phase_counts (P05:93, P06:57) and micro_phase_row_counts also sum to 150. Pack chaining is intact: CP439.next_pack_id=CP00-440, CP440.upstream=CP00-439, CP440.next=CP00-441, next_subphase RP14.P06.M03.S03. The partial first micro (M06 starts at S04 'Base document fixture', 19 rows) and partial last micro (M03 = 2 rows) are correctly modeled. Boundaries are sound: freezeCp440Result, SETTLEMENT_CORE_CP440_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (spread from CP439), the contract, and the manifest all attest writes_product_state:false, no_real_data/no_real_data:true, no settlement/origination/allocation/working-credit runtime, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, no cross-tenant access, and the full no-leak guard set. Production-ready posture does not overclaim: command-evidence and manifest declare production_ready:false with status implemented_pending_review / implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout; the code computes only production_ready_candidate (coverage.valid && slice.valid), which is explicitly separated from the final enterprise-trust claim that requires human approval. Authority boundary verified in code (claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false), contract, manifest (claude_not_final_approver:true, enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation:true), and review-request (read_only:true, tools Read/Grep/Glob). Hermes H14 patterns appear only as deterministic review-packet/receipt descriptors, not product runtime. CP178+ review hardening is satisfied: forbidden_review_evidence covers failed_authentication_run, zero_byte_output, malformed_json_output, fenced_json_output, tool_call_shaped_output, claude_final_approval_claim, and production_enterprise_trust_claim; readiness-report shows auth_preflight passed with 1456-byte valid JSON (not zero-byte/auth-failure). All evidence validation commands resolve to real wired npm scripts and are recorded exit_code 0. The post-review artifacts referenced by the manifest (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are not yet present, which is correct for this pre-adjudication review stage per the hardened_review_sequence and is not a defect. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
